queries.term: ("machine learning"[Title/Abstract] OR "deep learning"[Title/Abstract]) AND (genomics OR proteomics OR "drug discovery" OR "computational biology")

papers.title: Trends in the Application of Multiomics Based on Machine Learning in the Development of Probiotics.
papers.journal: Journal of agricultural and food chemistry
summary_bullets: Machine learning has emerged as an important analytical approach in probiotic research.
ML facilitates the integration of heterogeneous omics data to enable more systematic and quantitative probiotic development.
Experimental validation remains essential for ensuring biological relevance, despite limitations in capturing complex microbial and environmental interactions.

papers.title: A framework for identifying the polyploid complex in Rorippa (Brassicaceae): combining trait evolution, herbarium records, and machine learning.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Species identification in polyploid plants is challenging due to morphological continuity and genomic redundancy.
Seed arrangement, petal number, and genome size exhibited clear interspecific differentiation in the Rorippa dubia-indica complex.

papers.title: High MAP3K6 Expression in Spinal Cord Injury Tissues Enhances Neuronal Apoptosis: Insights from Multi-Omics Data Integrating WGCNA, Machine Learning, and Experimental Validation.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
4,621 differentially expressed genes were identified between spinal cord injury (SCI) and controls.
The green module showed the strongest positive correlation with SCI using WGCNA analysis.

papers.title: Genome characterization of cluster EK1 bacteriophages JessellCookie and MiamiPanther.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Phages JessellCookie and MiamiPanther were isolated from environmental samples.
The phages were isolated using Microbacterium foliorum as a host.

papers.title: Unraveling the genome of Epidendrum fulgens: demographic history and gene family dynamics in a resilient Neotropical orchid.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The first chromosome-scale genome assembly from the orchid genus Epidendrum was created using PacBio HiFi and Omni-C technologies.
The assembly comprised 12 chromosome-scale scaffolds, capturing 97.3% of BUSCO genes, with an N50 of 88.6 Mbp and L50 of 5.

papers.title: Possible cross‑talk between sarcopenia and obstructive sleep apnea revealed by WGCNA analysis and machine learning.
papers.journal: The aging male : the official journal of the International Society for the Study of the Aging Male
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The study identified 20 shared common genes associated with the comorbidity of sarcopenia and obstructive sleep apnea (OSA).
LGR6 and ARRDC2 were determined to be candidate hub genes for diagnosing OSA combined with sarcopenia.

papers.title: Post translational modifications as biomarkers of soil microbe responses to nano-pesticides.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Nano-pesticides can induce subtle disturbances in soil microbial function that are not captured by conventional indicators.
Post-translational modifications (PTMs) such as phosphorylation, acetylation, and ubiquitination regulate microbial stress responses under nanoparticle-induced stress.

papers.title: Validation of important genes and bioinformatics investigation of the relationship between ferroptosis and CVA16 infection.
summary_bullets: No abstract available.

papers.title: Uncertainty-aware hybrid deep generative framework for robust and explainable drug discovery.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:

A novel hybrid computational framework integrates Graph Convolutional Networks, Variational Autoencoders, and Uncertainty Aware Adaptive Multi-Objective Optimization-based Reinforcement Learning to generate drug molecules.

The proposed method prioritizes reliability by combining uncertainty awareness and explainability, reducing false positives and addressing challenges in drug discovery such as robustness and interpretability.

papers.title: Prosta-omics: machine learning-driven TPSA prediction and molecular modeling of ASPM-inhibitors for prostate cancer treatment.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Single-cell RNA sequencing enabled fine-grained clustering and differential gene expression analysis across malignant and non-malignant cell populations.
ASPM emerged as a statistically significant biomarker associated with proliferative tumor phenotypes, enriched in certain cell types.

papers.title: Integrating ensemble machine-learning and fibril docking to discover potent, novel triazole-naphthalene tau-aggregation inhibitors.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The study trained a stacked-ensemble QSAR model to predict human-tau aggregation inhibitors with high accuracy.
The model predicts that 16 previously unreported compounds (TND-1 to TND-15) have potency against tau-protein aggregation, with top-ranked candidates being TND-9, TND-15, and TND-5.

papers.title: Cell-free chromatin state tracing reveals disease origin and therapy responses.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The cf-EpiTracing platform profiles histone modifications in cell-free DNA from plasma to deconvolve cell types of origin.
The platform enabled unbiased identification of primary diseased tissues and other organ involvement, as well as stratification of B cell lymphoma subtypes.

papers.title: AI can write genomes - how long until it creates synthetic life?
summary_bullets: No abstract available.

papers.title: Cell-free DNA fragmentomes for noninvasive detection of liver cirrhosis and other diseases.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
A machine learning classifier detected early liver disease, advanced fibrosis, and cirrhosis with high sensitivity in separate discovery and validation cohorts.
Genome-wide fragmentome and methylome analyses revealed liver-derived and immune-mediated changes in cfDNA in individuals affected with liver disease.

papers.title: Deep learning linking mechanistic models to single-cell transcriptomics data reveals transcriptional bursting in response to DNA damage.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The study proposes a framework called DeepTX that connects mechanistic models with scRNA-seq data to reveal genome-wide transcriptional burst kinetics.
Fluctuations in transcriptional bursting induced by different DNA-damaging drugs were associated with distinct fate decisions, including differentiation and apoptosis/survival-related fates.

papers.title: Systemic Proteome Profiling to Differentiate Primary Glomerular Diseases.
papers.journal: Journal of the American Society of Nephrology : JASN
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Plasma proteome profiles were distinct among disease subtypes.
The machine learning model performed robustly in both discovery and validation cohorts, achieving an AUROC > 0.8 for differentiating certain GN subtypes.

papers.title: Decoding d- and l-Amino Acids: Data-Driven Recognition of Enantiomers and Post-Translational Modifications via Quantum Tunneling.
papers.journal: The journal of physical chemistry letters
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The authors develop a machine learning approach to identify d- and l-amino acid enantiomers.
Their method uses quantum tunneling to screen and detect isobaric isomers, methylation, phosphorylation, and ring formation in complex proteomes.

papers.title: In-Pocket 3D Graphs Enhance Ligand-Target Compatibility in Generative Small-Molecule Creation: A Dopamine D2 Receptor Model System.
papers.journal: The journal of physical chemistry. B
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The graph-based generative model encodes explicit 3D protein-ligand contacts.
Molecules generated with the 3D procedure are more compatible with the dopamine D2 receptor-binding pocket than those produced by a comparable 2D method.

papers.title: Deep generative molecular design and its value in modern drug discovery.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Neural network-based frameworks enable algorithms to propose molecules that satisfy complex pharmacological constraints.
Generative AI's greatest promise lies not in generating more molecules, but in generating better hypotheses and structures that are synthetically accessible and biologically plausible.

papers.title: miR-1290 serves as a potential biomarker for osteoporotic fracture and regulates osteoblast function by targeting KDM5A.
papers.journal: Journal of orthopaedic surgery and research
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Serum miR-1290 levels decrease with increasing osteoporotic severity.
miR-1290 promotes osteoblast proliferation and differentiation by targeting KDM5A.

papers.title: Identification of metabolism and inflammation-related biomarkers and potential drugs for schizophrenia based on comprehensive bioinformatics and machine learning.
summary_bullets: No abstract available.

papers.title: Genome-wide identification of castor bean OSCA genes and analysis of expression patterns under drought and salt stress.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
A total of 10 OSCA genes (RcOSCAs) were identified in castor bean, unevenly distributed on five chromosomes.
Phylogenetic analysis classified RcOSCA proteins into four distinct subfamilies and clarified their evolutionary relationships with homologs from other plants.

papers.title: Identification of the disulfidptosis-related gene G6PD as a potential biomarker and therapeutic target in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma by integrative bioinformatics analysis and experimental validation.
summary_bullets: No abstract available.

papers.title: Identification and characterization of Phi1 and Phi3 bacteriophages targeting Xylella fastidiosa subsp. pauca, the causal agent of olive quick decline in Italy.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Two phages, Xylella phage Phi1 and Phi3, were isolated and characterized as having potential as safe and suitable biocontrol agents.
Transmission electron microscopy revealed that Phi1 exhibited podovirus morphotype features while Phi3 showed siphovirus morphotype features, with efficient replication on Xf cells.

papers.title: Benzo[a]pyrene promotes gastric cancer progression via activation of the Correa cascade through modulation of the STAT3-TP53-MMP9 molecular axis.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The genes STAT3, TP53, and MMP9 were identified as core targets that drive the Correa cascade during gastric cancer development.
These genes exhibited synergistic expression patterns in tumors and showed a dose-dependent association with disease progression.

papers.title: Plasma proteomic profiles of lung volume-based phenotypes in Tobacco-Exposed Individuals Without Spirometric COPD.
papers.journal: Annals of the American Thoracic Society
summary_bullets: The lung volume-based pre-COPD phenotypes have distinct biological features reflected in their circulating proteome.
Among pre-COPD subgroups, those with a relatively elevated functional residual capacity-to-TLC ratio ([FRC/TLC]high) showed greater progression to GOLD ≥ 2 and PRISm.
Plasma proteomic analysis identified 165 upregulated and 145 downregulated proteins in [TLC]high TEPS compared with other groups.

papers.title: Proteomic profile of CSF obtained at the time of diagnosis determines amyotrophic lateral sclerosis progression and survival: CXCL7 levels in disease prognosis and survival.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The study identified a molecular signature in cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) that robustly distinguished ALS patients from controls and stratified patient subgroups by survival.

The signature was characterized by inflammation, downregulation of detoxification mechanisms, and synaptic dysregulation in aggressive disease forms, providing insights into the pathophysiology of ALS.

papers.title: [Mechanism of miR-199a-5p prevents migration and invasion of cholangiocarcinoma by targeting hypoxia-inducible factor 1α].
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the biomedical abstract:
miR-199a-5p expression was investigated in intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma (ICC) tumor tissues.
The study found a correlation between miR-199a-5p and hypoxia-inducible factor 1α (HIF-1α) expression levels and clinicopathological features of ICC patients.

papers.title: Intergenerational neurotoxicity mediated by maternal plasma metabolite profile remodeling following maternal atrazine exposure.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Maternal exposure to atrazine during pregnancy and lactation damages dopaminergic neurons in offspring.
The study reveals that maternal atrazine exposure remodels the maternal plasma metabolic profile, leading to intergenerational dopaminergic neurotoxicity.

papers.title: AlleleMiner: A long-read pipeline for gene-wise de novo allele phasing and variant detection in diploid citrus cultivars.
papers.journal: DNA research : an international journal for rapid publication of reports on genes and genomes
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The AlleleMiner pipeline phases diploid gene sequences directly from PacBio HiFi reads without relying on reference-based coordinate systems.
Across 18 citrus cultivars, the pipeline achieved an average phasing output of 91.5% of single-copy genes with a preference for ∼30× HiFi depth to recover heterozygous alleles.

papers.title: Linking protein aggregation and structural stability to predict pathogenic MYH7 variants via machine learning.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Disease-causing variants for Myosin Storage Myopathy generally destabilize the MYH7 α-helical coiled-coil domain.
Pathogenic variants cluster in locally unwound regions of the coiled-coil dimer, promoting dimer destabilization and aggregation.

papers.title: Assessment of In-Frame Indel Variants in an Unsolved Cohort of Inherited Retinal Diseases Using Machine Learning.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The performance of four machine learning (ML) tools for predicting the pathogenicity of in-frame insertions and deletions was compared.
MetaRNN-indel showed the best overall results among the compared ML tools.

papers.title: Genomics of Sable (Martes zibellina) × Pine Marten (Martes martes) Hybridization.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The study found a broad spectrum of hybrid types between sable (Martes zibellina) and pine marten (Martes martes) with mosaic recombinant chromosomes.
The analysis revealed mitochondrial DNA introgression from sables into most pine martens, indicating displacement of native pine marten mitochondrial sequences.

papers.title: Germline-specific nascent polypeptide-associated complex (NAC) subunits are predicted by bioinformatics tools to undergo phase separation.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The germline NAC (gNAC) proteins have an atypical amino acid composition and low-complexity repeats that can form cross-β structures.
gNAC proteins also have extended disordered regions that may undergo structuring upon oligomerization, suggesting a propensity for phase separation.

papers.title: L-Se-methylselenocysteine alleviates iron deposit-induced anxiety- and depression-like behaviors in chronic renal failure rats receiving iron therapy via Nrf2/GPX4 activation.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the biomedical abstract:
ESRD patients showed higher Beck Depression Inventory scores than healthy controls.
Increased magnetic susceptibility was found in the ventromedial prefrontal cortex and hippocampus, positively correlated with depression severity.

papers.title: Integrative chromosome-scale genome analysis of cupuassu provides insights into witches' broom disease resistance and expands genomic resources for Theobroma.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The genome of the WBD-resistant cupuassu genotype C174 is presented, featuring distinctive duplications, TE insertions, and coordinated defense gene expression.

Comparison with the susceptible genotype C1074 reveals differences in nucleotide-binding domain leucine-rich repeat receptors, mitogen-activated protein kinase components, transcription factors, and pathogenesis-related proteins.

papers.title: Leveraging Artificial Intelligence to Advance Bioinformatics in Africa: Opportunities, Challenges, and Ethical Considerations in Combating Antimicrobial Resistance.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Artificial intelligence (AI) can reduce turnaround times for antimicrobial-resistant (AMR) prediction from days to hours by leveraging whole-genome sequencing-based models.

AI techniques, such as machine learning and deep learning algorithms, can enhance genomic research by automating large-scale sequence dataset analysis, predicting resistance patterns, and modeling infection transmission dynamics.

papers.title: Identification of Genetic Variants Among Breast Cancer Patients and At-Risk Individuals: A Cohort Study in Sri Lanka.
papers.journal: Breast cancer : basic and clinical research
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
Variants were detected in several high- and moderate-penetrance genes, including BRCA1, BRCA2, PALB2, BRIP1, and MRE11A.
Among these variants, BRCA2 mutations and a PALB2 frameshift deletion were classified as pathogenic germline variants.

papers.title: Bioinformatic Analysis and Experimental Verification Reveal the Protective Role of Baicalein Against LPS-Induced Endothelial Cell Dysfunction.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
RNA-seq analysis revealed a set of differentially expressed genes (DEGs) shared between control and LPS-treated HUVECs.
Molecular docking and molecular dynamics simulation analyses showed that BAI exhibits strong binding affinity for key targets VCAM1 and PLCXD1.

papers.title: Elucidating the mechanisms of aristolochic acid-induced upper tract urothelial carcinoma: A multi-omics approach combining bioinformatics and computational modeling.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The study identified 97 shared potential targets of Aristolochic acids (AAs) and upper tract urothelial carcinoma (UTUC).
Molecular docking predicted high binding affinities of AA with five core genes: CASP3, EGFR, PARP1, PTGS2, and HSP90AA1.

papers.title: Unraveling the Phylogenetic Signal of Gene Expression from Single-cell RNA-seq Data.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
ScAllele, Monopogen, and Monovar consistently returned phylogenetically informative genotype calls.
These strategies provided precise signals for discriminating between tumor and normal cells within heterogeneous samples.

papers.title: A Real-Time, Programmable, and Multiplex Ligation Chain Reaction-MNAzyme Platform for Single-Nucleotide Variant Detection.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The LCR-MNAzyme system enables PCR-like real-time quantification of single-nucleotide variants.
The platform supports programmable and multiplex detection through reconfiguring the signal output module.

papers.title: DoBSeqWF: a framework for sensitive detection of individual genetic variation in pooled sequencing data.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
DoBSeq is a cost-effective method that uses 2D overlapping pool sequencing to enable individual-level rare variant detection.
The resulting data require a specialized workflow, which was developed into a Nextflow-based pipeline called DoBSeqWF for efficient and reproducible analysis.

papers.title: ANOMALY: a Snakemake pipeline for identifying NuMTs from long-read sequencing data.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The novel workflow ANOMALY detects nuclear mitochondrial DNA segments (NuMTs) from long-read sequencing data.
On 50 simulated datasets, the pipeline demonstrated high accuracy with a precision of 1.000 and a recall of 0.989.

papers.title: Dual Confinement-Enhanced Multiple Single Nucleotide Variant Detection at the Single-Particle Level.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the biomedical abstract:
A novel strategy was developed for multiple single nucleotide variants (SNVs) analysis at the single particle level using molecular and nanomaterial confinement.

The approach uses DNA tetrahedra to enhance sample background tolerance and X-shaped probes on DNA tetrahedra form high-stability recognition units that trigger a hybridization chain reaction (HCR) cascade.

papers.title: NGSTroubleFinder: a tool for detection and quantification of contamination and kinship across human NGS data.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
NGSTroubleFinder detects cross-sample contamination in whole-genome and transcriptome sequencing data.
The tool can identify sample swaps and mismatches between reported and inferred genetic and transcriptomic sexes.

papers.title: MobiCT: a UMI-based circulating tumor DNA analysis pipeline.
summary_bullets: Here are three bullet points summarizing the abstract:
The MobiCT pipeline detects ultra-low-frequency variants using unique molecular identifier (UMI) in cell-free DNA samples.
The pipeline consists of three stages: UMI deduplication, alignment to a reference genome, and variant calling.
